title: Treehouse Of Horror Xxiii

jokes.text: Don't worry. We got a guy here we really been fattening up.
jokes.setup: Mayan calendar predicts world destruction unless gods are appeased
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of fattening someone for sacrifice
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor with efficient setup and payoff, typical Simpsons historical parody

jokes.text: More cricket fajitas!
jokes.setup: Homer is the sacrifice victim being fattened
jokes.punchline: He's enthusiastically eating disgusting food
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - oblivious enthusiasm for food regardless of circumstances

jokes.text: Although I... I did kind of space out during orientation.
jokes.setup: Homer shocked to learn about sacrifice
jokes.punchline: Admits he wasn't paying attention when told
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific humor that perfectly captures Homer's obliviousness

jokes.text: Even though you know it's coming, when you see your own beating heart, try to act surprised, huh? It's some kid's first time.
jokes.setup: Priest offering words of comfort before sacrifice
jokes.punchline: Asking victim to fake surprise for trainee's benefit
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark absurdist humor - treating ritualistic murder like a workplace training exercise

jokes.text: What priest isn't?
jokes.setup: Marge asks if priest is interested in women
jokes.punchline: Priest casually admits sexual impropriety
jokes.explanation: Sharp, economical dark humor about religious hypocrisy

jokes.text: That whooshing sound is really turning me...
jokes.setup: Priest thinks he's about to have sex with Marge
jokes.punchline: Sound effect reveals he's being killed instead
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection gag with audio-visual payoff typical of Simpsons violence humor

jokes.text: Man, soccer's even boring for the ball.
jokes.setup: Bart as a soccer ball in Mayan ballgame
jokes.punchline: Meta-complaint about soccer being boring
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-humor with perfect Bart voice and excellent economy

jokes.text: And it will be Obama's fault!
jokes.setup: Ancient Mayan predicting world will end in 2012
jokes.punchline: Blaming future president Obama
jokes.explanation: Topical political humor with good anachronistic absurdity but somewhat predictable

jokes.text: Hey, uh, Marge, I'm setting my watch. What baktun is it? 13th! Oh!
jokes.setup: Homer treating Mayan calendar like regular time
jokes.punchline: Marge's shocked realization of the apocalyptic timing
jokes.explanation: Good callback that transitions from opening segment to main show

jokes.text: Welcome to the grand opening of our whatchamacallit, which, thanks to the lobbying of Lisa Simpson, we built instead of a new baseball stadium.
jokes.setup: Official opening ceremony for scientific facility
jokes.punchline: Mayor doesn't know what it is and blames Lisa for no stadium
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Quimby's incompetence and anti-intellectualism

jokes.text: At least the pennant works both ways.
jokes.setup: Particle accelerator instead of baseball stadium
jokes.punchline: Pennant can be used for either purpose
jokes.explanation: Small but clever visual detail about municipal priorities

jokes.text: Professor Frink will now throw a switch, which will either answer certain obscure questions of subatomic physics or destroy the universe.
jokes.setup: Scientific experiment ceremony
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of potential universal destruction
jokes.explanation: Great understated delivery of apocalyptic risk, typical Simpsons dark humor

jokes.text: Oh, my God particle!
jokes.setup: Scientific experiment begins
jokes.punchline: Frink's exclamation puns on 'God particle' (Higgs boson)
jokes.explanation: Excellent science pun perfectly suited to Frink's character and speech pattern

jokes.text: You can tell your grandchildren you were here when humanity finally learned that this accelerator is much too small to tell us anything important.
jokes.setup: Grand scientific moment with large crowd
jokes.punchline: Experiment is completely useless
jokes.explanation: Smart satirical comment on scientific funding and public expectations

jokes.text: Thanks a lot, Liser! That money could've been used for a war!
jokes.setup: Scientific experiment fails to produce results
jokes.punchline: Homer prefers war spending to science
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer anti-intellectualism with dark political satire

jokes.text: Aah! No one takes Willie's mop!
jokes.setup: Black hole forming and sucking things in
jokes.punchline: Willie's protective attachment to his mop
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor about Willie's working-class priorities

jokes.text: Take the mop! Take the mop!
jokes.setup: Willie initially protective of mop
jokes.punchline: Immediately gives up when faced with black hole
jokes.explanation: Good quick character reversal showing Willie's cowardice

jokes.text: I's rich! I's poor and shack-less.
jokes.setup: Black hole appears to give people money
jokes.punchline: Cletus gets money but loses his home
jokes.explanation: Perfect Cletus dialogue with good ironic reversal of fortune

jokes.text: Two stray dogs?! That was gonna be my next guess. Are you sure your next guess wasn't three stray dogs? Maybe.
jokes.setup: Lisa tells Homer there's a black hole in basement
jokes.punchline: Homer's absurdly incremental guessing pattern
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy showing Homer's simple-minded pattern recognition

jokes.text: Hey! Could it open a pretzel bag for me? Help a brother out, B.H.
jokes.setup: Scientific explanation of black holes
jokes.punchline: Homer wants to use cosmic phenomenon for snacks
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - reducing cosmic forces to personal convenience

jokes.text: Come on. You can't look at that infinitely dense little guy and not want to feed it something.
jokes.setup: Lisa warns against feeding the black hole
jokes.punchline: Homer anthropomorphizes black hole as cute pet
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy with Homer's childlike perspective on cosmic destruction

jokes.text: I'm nature's perfect choking machine!
jokes.setup: Homer interacting with black hole
jokes.punchline: Classic Homer-Bart choking dynamic
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons callback with good self-aware delivery

jokes.text: I know we shouldn't feed this, but grease brings raccoons.
jokes.setup: Marge disposing of grease near black hole
jokes.punchline: Worried about raccoons instead of cosmic destruction
jokes.explanation: Excellent Marge character moment with perfect domestic priority inversion

jokes.text: Quit bageling my English muffins!
jokes.setup: Black hole affecting Ned's breakfast
jokes.punchline: Ned's food anxiety with made-up verb
jokes.explanation: Creative wordplay perfectly suited to Ned's neurotic food concerns

jokes.text: Okay, it's possible someone may have started a business called Magic Craphole Waste Removal, but it wasn't me.
jokes.setup: Homer denying responsibility for black hole growth
jokes.punchline: Admits to exploiting cosmic phenomenon for profit
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer entrepreneur moment with great vulgar business name

jokes.text: I did it! I hit my first home run! No...! I'm still proud of you, buddy.
jokes.setup: Bart hits baseball that triggers black hole disaster
jokes.punchline: Homer's parental pride despite apocalyptic consequences
jokes.explanation: Perfect parental moment with excellent dark irony about misplaced priorities

jokes.text: You saw that I had the money, right?
jokes.setup: World ending due to black hole disaster
jokes.punchline: Homer concerned about payment for pizza
jokes.explanation: Great character comedy showing Homer's skewed priorities during apocalypse

jokes.text: Our most popular magazine is your Presidents' Day Mattress Blowout insert.
jokes.setup: Aliens describing Earth's 'gifts' to their dimension
jokes.punchline: Treating junk mail as high culture
jokes.explanation: Clever satire of consumer culture and alien perspective on human civilization

jokes.text: And we listen to all our music on the most wonderful device ever created, the Zune.
jokes.setup: Aliens praising Earth technology
jokes.punchline: Failed Microsoft device treated as pinnacle of innovation
jokes.explanation: Good topical humor about technology failures and alien ignorance

jokes.text: remember me as a hero. Dad, you forgot to pick me up after Little League. You tossed your car keys in my bean plant. A hero!
jokes.setup: Homer wants to be remembered as hero
jokes.punchline: Immediate evidence of his irresponsible parenting
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic juxtaposition showing Homer's self-delusion

jokes.text: Note to self-- edit out my lies.
jokes.setup: Homer filming documentary-style footage
jokes.punchline: Admits to lying on camera while camera is recording
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-humor about found footage genre and Homer's incompetence

jokes.text: We have stairs?
jokes.setup: Homer running upstairs during emergency
jokes.punchline: Doesn't know basic layout of his own house
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer obliviousness moment with excellent economy

jokes.text: Sometimes a mysterious, invisible being from Hell waits for a family to go to sleep then kills them. Now, go to bed.
jokes.setup: Bart asking about supernatural events
jokes.punchline: Marge's matter-of-fact description followed by bedtime instruction
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark parenting humor with perfect deadpan delivery

jokes.text: Ooh, I can't wait for tonight's massacre.
jokes.setup: Homer setting up cameras to catch supernatural activity
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic anticipation of family's death
jokes.explanation: Good dark Homer moment showing his misplaced entertainment priorities

jokes.text: It's Moe with wings!
jokes.setup: Demonic creature appears
jokes.punchline: Homer compares to local bartender
jokes.explanation: Good character-based observation with efficient visual comedy

jokes.text: Hail Satan, the Minister of Sinister, Rude Dude with a Bad 'Tude.
jokes.setup: Young Marge and sisters summoning devil
jokes.punchline: Silly rhyming nicknames for Satan
jokes.explanation: Creative wordplay that captures teenage irreverence toward evil

jokes.text: Three-way. Hmm. You, me and Marge? Mmm, demon, demon, you.
jokes.setup: Homer asking for alternative deal with demon
jokes.punchline: Sexual proposition and Homer's confused counting
jokes.explanation: Adult humor with characteristic Homer confusion, slightly cheap but in character

jokes.text: The only way to buy that comic book for 25 cents is to go back to 1974. That's impossible! Or is it?
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy explaining inflation
jokes.punchline: Revelation of time machine availability
jokes.explanation: Good setup for time travel plot with typical Comic Book Guy delivery

jokes.text: Here, kid, hold my keys while I grab some lunch.
jokes.setup: Frink just invented most powerful device in history
jokes.punchline: Casually leaves it with child for lunch break
jokes.explanation: Perfect academic absent-mindedness with excellent ironic juxtaposition

jokes.text: Now to get my comic book and explore a world where no one's mad at George Lucas.
jokes.setup: Bart going to 1974
jokes.punchline: Reference to pre-prequel Star Wars era
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference with clever historical perspective

jokes.text: Eh, pretty good. A little preachy.
jokes.setup: Young Homer just saw original Star Wars
jokes.punchline: Dismissive review of classic film
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment with excellent economy and cultural commentary

jokes.text: It's Homer! Before his boobs came in!
jokes.setup: Adult Bart meeting young Homer and Barney
jokes.punchline: Barney's crude observation about Homer's aging
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific crude humor, slightly cheap but fits Barney perfectly

jokes.text: I'm your unwanted son from the future who killed all your fun!
jokes.setup: Young Homer asks who adult Bart is
jokes.punchline: Bart's brutally honest self-description
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark self-aware humor about parent-child dynamics with great economy

jokes.text: Or my life might be a hell of a lot better! Ma'am, whatever you do, do not marry that ape.
jokes.setup: Bart realizes consequences of changing timeline
jokes.punchline: Immediately sabotages his own existence
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's impulsive self-destructive tendencies

jokes.text: Ice cream with cookie dough?! Unnecessarily big TVs! Thursday Night Football? And the globe feels so warm!
jokes.setup: Young Homer experiencing future world
jokes.punchline: Amazed by modern conveniences and global warming
jokes.explanation: Good time travel humor with environmental commentary and character consistency

jokes.text: Why would I suck smoothie when I can suck face with you, Mrs. Artie Ziff?
jokes.setup: Marge offering smoothie to son in alternate timeline
jokes.punchline: Bart's inappropriate romantic advance due to different parentage
jokes.explanation: Decent alternate timeline humor but somewhat uncomfortable given the context

jokes.text: My dad is Artie Ziff? Indeed! Which makes you Barty Ziff. Oy, caramba!
jokes.setup: Bart discovers alternate timeline parentage
jokes.punchline: Name combination and shocked reaction
jokes.explanation: Decent name-based humor with good character reaction

jokes.text: Will that be haw-haw, sir? Give yourself a wedgie. Excellent choice.
jokes.setup: Rich Bart has own bully butler
jokes.punchline: Professional bullying service with formal politeness
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd concept of formalized bullying as luxury service

jokes.text: You're just like Indiana Jones, son. A role played by Richard Dreyfuss in our universe.
jokes.setup: Homer complimenting Bart in rich timeline
jokes.punchline: Different casting in alternate universe
jokes.explanation: Good alternate universe humor with interesting casting choice reference

jokes.text: Crush them... in the name of Jebus!
jokes.setup: Multiple Homers fighting
jokes.punchline: Caveman Homer's mispronunciation of Jesus
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons callback with good primitive character integration

jokes.text: I was supposed to be with all of you!
jokes.setup: Marge realizing she married wrong man
jokes.punchline: Wants to be with ALL versions of Homer
jokes.explanation: Good dark polygamy humor with interesting character development

jokes.text: That's Renaissance Homer. Well, cardinal or cannibal, I wish that 20 years ago, I had chosen love.
jokes.setup: Marge correcting Bart about caveman Homer
jokes.punchline: Alliterative confusion and romantic declaration
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay with touching character moment about regret and choice

jokes.text: Make Egyptian Slave Homer do it. It's always me.
jokes.setup: Multiple Homers deciding who takes out garbage
jokes.punchline: Slave Homer's resigned complaint
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor about workplace dynamics across timelines

tmdb_overview: A black hole threatens to swallow Springfield, Homer tries to find the source of paranormal activity, and a "Back to the Future" parody in which Bart keeps Marge from marrying Homer.
